complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the ETNews crawler implementation into: (1) HTTP client setup with requests library and error handling, (2) BeautifulSoup HTML parsing for section listing page, (3) Article link extraction and URL pattern matching, (4) Article detail page fetching and parsing (title/date/body), (5) Article data model and return structure. Each subtask should be testable independently.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Moderate complexity due to: web scraping fragility (HTML parsing with BeautifulSoup), Korean text encoding handling, network error handling with retry logic, and pattern matching for ETNews URL structure. Greenfield implementation but straightforward architecture. Main challenges: understanding ETNews HTML structure, handling network failures gracefully, and extracting correct Korean text from article pages.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement TF-IDF keyword extraction with KoNLPy
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down Korean keyword extraction into: (1) KoNLPy and dependencies installation/setup (JPype1 for JVM), (2) Korean text tokenization with Okt tokenizer, (3) Stopword filtering and token cleanup, (4) TF-IDF scoring implementation with scikit-learn, (5) Top-N keyword selection logic, (6) Error handling for JVM initialization and encoding issues. Focus on Korean-specific challenges.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Higher complexity due to: KoNLPy's JVM dependency (JPype1 setup can be finicky), Korean morphological analysis complexity, proper Korean text encoding (UTF-8), stopword filtering for Korean, and TF-IDF calculation for single-document context. The JVM requirement adds setup complexity, especially for CI/CD environments. Korean NLP is more complex than English due to morphology.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down TextRank summarization into: (1) sumy library installation and Korean language support setup, (2) Korean sentence tokenization preprocessing, (3) TextRank algorithm configuration and execution, (4) Post-processing (sentence ordering, joining) and fallback handling for edge cases. Include testing with various article lengths.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Moderate complexity. TextRank via sumy is relatively straightforward compared to keyword extraction. Main challenges: Korean sentence boundary detection, ensuring sentence order preservation, handling edge cases (very short articles), and fallback strategies. Less complex than task 2 because sumy abstracts the algorithm complexity and Korean support is built-in.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down post generation into: (1) Jekyll filename convention implementation (YYYY-MM-DD-slug.md with python-slugify for Korean→ASCII), (2) YAML front matter template creation with proper escaping, (3) Markdown body template with keywords/summary/attribution, (4) File I/O with UTF-8 encoding and directory creation, (5) Integration testing with Jekyll local preview. Ensure compatibility with existing minima theme.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Moderate complexity. Requires understanding Jekyll's strict filename/front matter conventions, Korean slug generation (python-slugify), proper YAML escaping for Korean titles, UTF-8 file handling, and ensuring compatibility with the existing minima theme. The template itself is simple, but getting all the details right (dates, slugs, YAML syntax) requires care. Integration testing with Jekyll preview is critical.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement article deduplication and state tracking
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down state management into: (1) JSON state file structure design and initialization, (2) StateManager class with load/save/query methods, (3) Article ID deduplication logic and filtering, (4) State file corruption recovery and atomic updates. Ensure state file is git-committable and human-readable.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Lower-moderate complexity. Straightforward JSON file-based state tracking with clear data structure. Main challenges: ensuring atomic updates, handling corrupted JSON gracefully, and integrating with git commits. No database complexity, just simple file I/O with JSON. The design is intentionally simple to minimize maintenance burden.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Create orchestrator script to run end-to-end pipeline
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the orchestrator into: (1) Logging setup and configuration, (2) Pipeline flow coordination (load state → crawl → filter → extract → generate → save), (3) Per-article error handling and retry logic, (4) Dry-run mode implementation for testing, (5) Exit code and failure reporting, (6) Integration testing with real ETNews data and state persistence across runs.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Moderate-high complexity due to orchestration of multiple components (tasks 1-5), error handling across the entire pipeline, ensuring graceful degradation (skip failed articles but continue processing), logging for observability, and implementing dry-run mode for testing. This is the integration point where all pieces come together, so bugs from any component surface here. Requires careful error handling to avoid losing work.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Create Python dependencies file and project structure
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down project setup into: (1) Directory structure creation (scripts/, data/, __init__.py files), (2) requirements.txt with pinned versions for all dependencies (requests, beautifulsoup4, konlpy, scikit-learn, sumy, python-slugify, PyYAML), (3) .gitignore updates for Python artifacts and documentation of JVM/Java requirement for KoNLPy.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Low complexity. Standard Python project setup with well-defined dependencies. Main work is: creating directory structure, listing and pinning dependency versions, and documenting the KoNLPy Java requirement. No algorithmic complexity, just configuration and documentation. The JVM dependency for KoNLPy is noted but installation is straightforward.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Build GitHub Actions workflow for daily automation
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down GitHub Actions workflow into: (1) Workflow file creation with cron schedule and workflow_dispatch trigger, (2) Python and Java environment setup (actions/setup-python, actions/setup-java for KoNLPy), (3) Dependency caching strategy with actions/cache, (4) Pipeline script execution step, (5) Git configuration and conditional commit logic (only if changes exist), (6) Push with proper permissions and [skip ci] tag, (7) Testing with manual trigger and verifying GitHub Pages rebuild.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Higher complexity due to: GitHub Actions YAML syntax and workflow orchestration, Java + Python dual environment setup (for KoNLPy JVM dependency), caching strategy for faster runs, conditional commit logic (git diff check), proper permissions configuration, and ensuring GitHub Pages triggers correctly. Testing requires understanding GitHub Actions debugging and monitoring. The dual-language runtime (Python + Java) adds setup complexity.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Add configuration file for pipeline parameters
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down configuration system into: (1) YAML config file structure design with all tunable parameters (crawler, extraction, post generation settings), (2) Config loading and validation logic with PyYAML, (3) Default value fallback handling, (4) Integration into orchestrator and extraction modules with parameter passing. Test with different config values.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Low-moderate complexity. Creating a YAML config file is straightforward, but requires: designing a clear schema, implementing validation, providing sensible defaults, and threading config values through the entire pipeline (orchestrator → crawler → extractor → generator). Testing different configurations adds complexity. The config system enables future extensibility but must be designed carefully upfront.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Add documentation and local testing instructions
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down documentation into: (1) README.md with project overview and architecture diagram, (2) Setup and installation instructions (Python, Java, dependencies), (3) Local testing guide (running pipeline manually, Jekyll preview, dry-run mode), (4) GitHub Actions usage and troubleshooting section, (5) Inline code documentation (docstrings for all functions, comments for complex logic).
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Low-moderate complexity. Documentation is important but straightforward. Main work is: writing clear setup instructions (especially for KoNLPy/Java requirement), creating local testing guide, documenting the GitHub Actions workflow, and adding inline code comments/docstrings. The challenge is making it comprehensive and clear for future maintainers. Testing the documentation with a fresh clone ensures accuracy.
